title: Social Transformation
description: This exercise will cover the social impacts of industrialization through the theme SIO. It will have two types of questions: reform movements and changes to social structures. Each of these types will scale 5 items, for a total of 12.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>In the condition of the proletariat, those of old society at large are already virtually swamped. The proletarian is without property; his relation to his wife and children has no longer anything in common with the bourgeois family relations; modern industry labour, modern subjection to capital, the same in England as in France, in America as in Germany, has stripped him of every trace of national character. Law, morality, religion, are to him so many bourgeois prejudices, behind which lurk in ambush just as many bourgeois interests. 

Source: Karl Marx, *The Communist Manifesto*, 1847. 

**Which of the following conditions from 1750 to 1900 CE gave rise to the developments described in the above excerpt?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Capitalists made profits off of labor, but wages stayed low.
Business owners created agricultural jobs in rural areas.
Peasants organized themselves in order to revolt against the feudal system.
Many women were empowered by new jobs in factories.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>We hold these truths to be self-evident; that all men and women are created equal; that they are endowed by their Creator with certain inalienable rights; that among these are life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness; that to secure these rights governments are instituted…

Source: Declaration of Sentiments, Written at the Seneca Falls Convention, USA, 1848. 

**Which of the following inspired reform movements like the one illustrated by the passage above?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise in evangelical Christianity
the abolition of property requirements to vote
the abolition of child labor
the rise in literacy rates

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>There were the beef-luggers, who carried two-hundred-pound quarters into the refrigerator-cars; a fearful kind of work, that began at four o'clock in the morning, and that wore out the most powerful men in a few years…
>
>There were the wool-pluckers, whose hands went to pieces even sooner than the hands of the pickle men; for the pelts of the sheep had to be painted with acid to loosen the wool, and then the pluckers had to pull out this wool with their bare hands, till the acid had eaten their fingers off. There were those who made the tins for the canned meat; and their hands, too, were a maze of cuts, and each cut represented a chance for blood poisoning.

Source: Upton Sinclair, *The Jungle*, 1906. 

**During the Industrial Revolution, attitudes such as the one expressed in the excerpt above contributed most directly to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: legislation that improved working conditions
court cases that resulted in victory for laborers
philanthropists donating money to fix factory conditions
executive action that addressed unsanitary living conditions in cities

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an illustration of the challenges faced by the global labor movement from 1750 to 1900 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Many workers moved around the world either involuntarily as slaves or voluntarily as migrant workers and indentured servants.
Many workers were more concerned with labor movements for people of their own nationality than those in other countries.
Workers in Asia and Africa did not experience the same hardships that were faced by laborers in factories in Europe and the Americas.
Workers in Asia and Africa were increasingly isolated from Europeans and those in Latin America.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The spread of disease in European and American cities prompted which of the following developments from 1750 to 1900 CE?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: sewer and clean water systems
increased wages
air conditioned ventilation systems
suburban sprawl

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.** 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Developments such as the one shown in the image above led to which of the following reforms during the Industrial Revolution?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Photo of a small child standing barefoot between large industrial machines.
options.caption: Child Laborer, Newberry, S.C. 1908. Photographer: Lewis W. Hine. Public Domain.

choices.content: mandatory public education
workplace dress codes
minimum wages
government inspections of factories

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following most accurately depicts a change to social structures brought on by industrialization?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the development of class consciousness
the creation of a ruling class
the formation of a peasant class
the expansion of global awareness

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>The [Factory Inquiry of 1833] Commissioners mention a crowd of cripples who appeared before them, who clearly owed their distortion to the long working hours. This distortion usually consists of a curving of the spinal column and legs…I have seldom traversed Manchester without meeting three or four … [cripples], suffering from precisely the same distortions of the spinal columns and legs…
>
>In cases in which a stronger constitution, better food, and other more favourable circumstances enabled the young operative to resist this effect of a barbarous exploitation, we find, at least, pain in the back, hips, and legs, swollen joints, varicose veins, and large, persistent ulcers in the thighs and calves. These afflictions are almost universal among the operatives…

Source: A German visitor to England named Friedrich Engels, *The Condition of the Working Class in England,* 1844. 

**The excerpt above reflects what shift in British society during the Industrial Revolution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The working class saw themselves and their lives as different from the wealthy.
The government decided to enact broad legislation that protected workers in factories.
Industrial capitalists created initiatives to decrease the gap between employers and employees.
Philanthropists created charities that were dedicated to improving working conditions in factories.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best summarizes the impact that the Industrial Revolution had on gender roles in Europe and the Americas?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The need for labor brought women into the workforce, who began advocating for expanded rights.
Gender roles remained largely unchanged, with most women’s labor remaining domestic.
As a result of their participation in the workforce, women were automatically granted equal rights.
Women used their roles in factories to advocate for better working conditions for children.

assessment_items.question_content:
**As European influence increased in Asia, some aspects of traditional Chinese culture began to change. Which of the following best explains how European influence among wealthy Chinese women differed from those in the working class from 1750 to 1900 CE?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Wealthy Chinese women adopted European fashions while working class women were hired as cheap labor on plantations and in factories.
Public roles and educational opportunities expanded for both wealthy and working-class Chinese women, but more so for wealthy women.
Working class Chinese women saw an improvement in working conditions that were advocated for by wealthy Chinese women.
Wealthy Chinese women earned the right to vote while working class women had to wait another fifty years for suffrage.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes gender roles in independence movements from 1750 to 1900 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Men and women in Africa and Latin America took on roles as labor organizers in the fight against imperialism.
Men in Africa and Latin America excluded women from labor organizations as they fought against imperialism.
Indigenous men and women in the Caribbean overthrew British colonizers in favor of Dutch fruit companies.
Women across the globe used their roles in the home to undermine the power of industrial capitalists.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.** 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following is a limitation of the reform movements that addressed the problem depicted in the image above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Photo of a small child standing barefoot between large industrial machines.
options.caption: Child Laborer, Newberry, S.C. 1908. Photographer: Lewis W. Hine. Public Domain.

choices.content: Child labor continued in colonies.
Labor unions were made illegal.
Women were paid significantly less than men.
Sixteen-hour days were only reduced twice a week.
